name: Reeflex Demo 4 - Nothing Gets Through (terminal DENY)

nodes.name: When clicking 'Execute workflow'
nodes.notes: Stand-in for whatever actually triggers this (a schedule, a webhook, an agent tool call).

nodes.name: Systemic schema change (irreversible+systemic+prod)
nodes.notes: Stand-in for your real upstream data. count=1 on purpose: R3 does not care about magnitude at all, only the axes -- a single DROP SCHEMA is just as systemic as a thousand-row wipe. This is what makes R3 different from R2 (demo1/3): it is not a bigger version of a holdable action, it is a different CATEGORY of action.

nodes.name: Reeflex Gate
nodes.notes: irreversible + systemic + production trips R3 (reeflex.policy/irreversible_systemic_prod) -- deny, NO hold_id, NO expires_ts (see reeflex-core README: 'irreversible_systemic_prod is resolvable by no principal -- its deny is terminal'). There is nothing downstream to resolve because reeflex-core never creates a hold for this rule in the first place.

nodes.name: Unexpected allow (should never fire for R3)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER - wired for completeness/robustness. Should not fire with these axes.

nodes.name: Unexpected hold (should never fire for R3 - no hold is ever created)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER - wired for completeness/robustness. R3 is a direct deny; unlike R2/R5 it never goes through the Held output, so there is no hold_id to resolve for this action, ever.

nodes.name: Terminal DENY - not resolvable by any principal
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER - replace with a real alert node. This is where every run of this demo ends up: rule reeflex.policy/irreversible_systemic_prod, reason 'irreversible systemic change in production is not allowed even with approval'. No human, agent, or automation principal can approve this -- see demo4-README.md for the freeze (REEFLEX_FREEZE) half of this story, which cannot be shown against the shared api-dev endpoint.

main.node: Unexpected allow (should never fire for R3)
Unexpected hold (should never fire for R3 - no hold is ever created)
Terminal DENY - not resolvable by any principal

meta.description: Reeflex n8n demo 4/5 (examples/n8n/, see top-level README.md for the index + credential setup). Teaches: R3 (irreversible+systemic+production) is a TERMINAL deny -- no hold is ever created, no principal can approve it (reeflex-core README: 'irreversible_systemic_prod is resolvable by no principal'). Live and honest against the shared api-dev eval endpoint. Does NOT attempt to demonstrate REEFLEX_FREEZE (the operator-side kill-switch) -- that env var lives on the core server, not something an importer of this workflow can flip on a shared endpoint; see demo4-README.md for how to see it against your own core.
